description: Client-visible state model for NC1, including safety and test-mode semantics.

states.meaning: System is not actively armed for motion/action and accepts setup/config operations.
states.client_visible_semantics: Default target state after commissioning commit or controlled stop.

states.meaning: System is enabled for operational commands under configured safeguards.
states.client_visible_semantics: Operational commands may be accepted subject to runtime checks.

states.meaning: System is in explicit testing context with restricted command surface.
states.client_visible_semantics: Only allowed test and safety commands should be issued.

states.meaning: System detected safety-critical condition and enforced protective behavior.
states.client_visible_semantics: Clients should stop issuing non-recovery commands and surface blockers.

states.meaning: Required control/communication signal degraded below policy threshold.
states.client_visible_semantics: State may transition to failsafe or safe based on policy.

modifiers.description: Indicates stop condition remains active until explicit reset/ack policy is satisfied.
Indicates commissioning workflow currently constrains command/state transitions.

command_driven_transitions.notes: Runtime may enter failsafe instead if stop execution encounters safety fault.
Requires extension capability support and no active failsafe blockers.
May require additional safety acknowledgement in some implementations.

assumption_limits: State transition acceptance is subject to runtime policy and safety evaluation.
Clients must not assume deterministic transition latency.
Implementations may expose additional internal substates that map to these public states.
